summary: Feature request to support SkiaSharp as a Blazor Extension enabling use on both client-side (WASM) and server-side Blazor deployment models. Client-side WASM support was delivered via PR #1811 and the SkiaSharp.Views.Blazor package; server-side Blazor rendering remains unimplemented.

stepsToReproduce: Add SKCanvasView component to a Razor page
Run the app — throws NotSupportedException: SkiaSharp currently only works on Web Assembly

repoLinks.description: Mattleibow's prototype using SKPicture serialization over SignalR for server-side Blazor
Early prototype rendering images on server and sending to client
Sample repo for client-side Blazor WASM support
Reference implementation of a Blazor Extension pattern for HTML5 Canvas

versionAnalysis.relevanceReason: Client-side WASM support was shipped in SkiaSharp 2.88+, but server-side Blazor support has never been implemented. The SkiaSharp.Views.Blazor package still only works on WebAssembly.

fixStatus.reason: Client-side WASM was delivered via PR #1811 (merged Oct 2021). Server-side Blazor rendering was prototyped but never formally added to the package. The current code has [SupportedOSPlatform("browser")] and uses WASM-only JS interop APIs.

analysis.summary: Original request (March 2020) was for SkiaSharp to work as a Blazor Extension — supporting both client WASM and server-side Blazor hosting models. The client WASM portion was delivered in October 2021 via PR #1811 (SkiaSharp.Views.Blazor package). Server-side remains unimplemented. The maintainer (mattleibow) prototyped server-side rendering using SKPicture serialization over SignalR in an external repo, but this was never productized. Issue #1921 was closed as a duplicate of this issue in November 2024.
analysis.rationale: Classified as feature-request because the issue explicitly asks for new functionality (Blazor Extension pattern for server-side rendering). The client-side WASM portion was delivered, making this partially complete. The remaining server-side feature requires a fundamentally different architecture: rendering on the server and transmitting results to the client, or serializing draw commands for client-side replay. This is a significant design effort, not a simple bug fix.

keySignals.text: [FEATURE] Support SkiaSharp as a Blazor Extension
keySignals.interpretation: Explicitly marked as a feature request by the reporter.

keySignals.text: The PR is almost ready: #1811 ... I have merged #1811
keySignals.interpretation: Client-side WASM support was delivered, addressing half of the original request.

keySignals.text: SkiaSharp.Views.Blazor package still works in Blazor WebAssembly apps only
keySignals.interpretation: Last substantive comment confirms server-side support is still missing.
Maintainer considers this the canonical issue for Blazor server-side support.

codeInvestigation.finding: SKCanvasView is decorated with [SupportedOSPlatform("browser")] — explicitly WASM-only. Uses ElementReference, JS interop, and GCHandle for pixel buffer transfer, all of which require in-process browser execution.

Pre-.NET 7 path throws NotSupportedException("SkiaSharp currently only works on Web Assembly") when IJSRuntime is not IJSInProcessRuntime (i.e., server-side Blazor). .NET 7+ path uses JSHost.ImportAsync which is also WASM-only.
<SupportedPlatform Include="browser" /> confirms browser-only targeting. Project also references SkiaSharp.NativeAssets.WebAssembly, further anchoring to WASM.
All rendering goes through HTML Canvas JS interop (putImageData, requestAnimationFrame). Server-side Blazor cannot use these APIs synchronously — would need a completely different rendering pipeline.

nextQuestions: Would server-side rendering via base64-encoded PNG images (as prototyped in BlazorSkiaSharp) be acceptable performance-wise for production use?
Should the SKPicture serialization approach (draw commands over SignalR) be pursued for lower-latency server-side scenarios?
With .NET 8+ Blazor Auto render mode, does the scenario change — could interactive rendering start on server then switch to WASM?
What is the demand level for server-side-only Blazor scenarios vs hybrid Auto mode?

resolution.hypothesis: Server-side Blazor support requires a fundamentally different rendering approach: either server-side image generation sent as encoded images, or SKPicture serialization of draw commands replayed on the client. Both approaches were prototyped by the maintainer but never productized.

proposals.title: Server-side image rendering via encoded images
proposals.description: Render on server using standard SkiaSharp (not the Blazor package), encode as PNG/JPEG, and send to client as tag via Blazor's normal component model. This was prototyped in mattleibow/SkiaSharpBlazorComponents.

proposals.title: SKPicture serialization over SignalR
proposals.description: Record draw commands as SKPicture on server, serialize and send over SignalR to client where they are replayed on a WASM SkiaSharp canvas. Prototyped in mattleibow/BlazorSkiaSharp. Lower bandwidth than images but requires client WASM support.

proposals.title: Blazor Auto render mode integration
proposals.description: With .NET 8+ Blazor Auto render mode, start with server-side image rendering and seamlessly transition to client WASM rendering. Would require the Blazor views to detect hosting model and switch rendering strategy.

resolution.recommendedProposal: Server-side image rendering via encoded images
resolution.recommendedReason: Simplest approach that works today with existing SkiaSharp server capabilities. Render-to-image is a well-understood pattern and was already prototyped by the maintainer. Users can implement this now without waiting for a formal package.

actionability.reason: Partially delivered (client WASM works), but the server-side Blazor feature remains valid and requested. Significant design work needed — not a quick fix. The maintainer has acknowledged this as the canonical tracking issue by closing #1921 as a duplicate.

actions.description: Summarize current state and provide server-side workaround guidance
